REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFFS ERIC BOICE, VINETTA BOICE AND YOWIE STROMBERG'S AMENDED MOTION FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION IS DENIED. THE CLASS, AS PROPOSED, IS NOT SUSCEPTIBLE TO CLASS CERTIFICATION. (THE COURT NOTES THAT PROPOSED CLASS AS DEFINED IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION IS DIFFERENT FROM THE PROPOSED CLASS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT. ADDITIONALLY, THE NOTICE OF MOTION DOES NOT CONTAIN A CLASS DEFINITION AT ALL). PLAINTIFF'S FAIL TO SHOW NUMEROCITY AND COMMON ISSUES, UNIFORM POLICY OR PATTERN OF REQUIRING OCCUPANTS TO CHECK OUT/REREGISTER FOR THE PURPOSE OF MAINTAINING TRANSIENT STATUS. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE PROPOSED CLASS REPRESENTATIVES QUALIFY AS "AN OCCUPANT" UNDER CC 1940.1. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFFS ERIC BOICE, VINETTA BOICE AND YOWIE STROMBERG'S MOTION FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION - THE MOTION FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION IS DENIED. INDIVIDUAL ISSUES PREDOMINATE AS TO THE ACTUAL DAMAGES OF EACH CLASS MEMBER AND THE REASONS WHY EACH MEMBER VACATED EITHER ONE DAY AFTER MOVING IN OR TWENTY EIGHT DAYS LATER. AN INDIVIDUAL ACTION WOULD BE BETTER THAN A CLASS ACTION GIVEN THESE PROOF ISSUES. AN INDIVIDUAL HAS AN INCENTIVE TO BRING HIS/HER OWN CLAIM GIVEN THE ABILITY TO RECOVER STATUTORY AND ACTUAL DAMAGES. ERIC BOICE AND YOWIE STROMBERG ARE NOT PROPER CLASS REPRESENTATIVES BECAUSE THEY DO NOT APPEAR TO HAVE ANY RELATIONSHIP WITH THE LANDLORD AND WERE MERELY LIVING AT THE PREMISES. THE PROPOSED CLASS IS OVERBROAD AS IT DATES BACK TO 2008. THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS THREE YEARS PURSUANT TO CCP 338 AND THE CONTINUING VIOLATION DOCTRINE DOES NOT APPLY. THAT IS A DOCTRINE IN EMPLOYMENT LAW AND CANNOT BE USED TO REVIVE STALE CLAIMS IN THIS CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. Notice Of Plaintiffs' Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint, For An Order Dismissing Class Claims, And Approval Of Notice To Class Members
Off Calendar Jun-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Tyson Redenbarger, Esq. 415-318-5709 of Hooshmand Law Group for Plaintiff ERIC BOICE, VINETTA BOICE, YOWIE STROMBERG.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ERIC BOICE, VINETTA BOICE, YOWIE STROMBERG'S Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint, For An Order Dismissing Class Claims, And Approval Of Notice To Class Members.

Plaintiffs' motion for leave to file a second amended complaint, for an order dismissing class claims, and for approval of notice to class members is granted in its entirety. Defendants Harcourt Group, LLC and Sojourn Properties, Inc. do not object to the dismissal of the class claims and in light of the affirmance of the denial of class certification and disapproval of the proposed class settlement, dismissing the class claims is the proper course. Plaintiffs' proposed notice to the class is factually accurate and reasonably notifies the recipients that they "may" but are not required to contact the Hooshmand Law Group, the firm that quite obviously knows quite a bit about the claims in this case. There is nothing unethical or improper about the statements in the notice about contacting the Hooshmand Law Group or another lawyer. Permitting plaintiffs to file an amended complaint adding Jennifer Hawkins as a plaintiff and four new causes of action comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings, particularly where, as here, no trial date has been set and the case is in the early stages of discovery. Nor have defendants identified any legal prejudice they would suffer by plaintiffs being permitted to file their proposed second amended complaint. The fact that Ms. Hawkins is being added by amendment instead of by joinder, intervention or consolidation with a separately filed lawsuit by Ms. Hawkins is immaterial and is not a reason to deny leave to file a second amended complaint. The face of the proposed second amended complaint and the prior orders in this case do not show that the claims sought to be alleged by Ms. Hawkins are time-barred. Per the American Pipe line of cases, the statute of limitations on Ms. Hawkins' claims were tolled from the date this case was filed until the remittitur issued by the Court of Appeal after its affirmance of the denial of plaintiff's motion for class certification. The California case law cited by defendants purporting that Ms. Hawkins may not avail herself of American Pipe tolling is inapposite. Falk v. Children's Hospital Los Angeles (2015) 237 Cal. App. 4th 1454 dealt with the situation where a party sought to bring a second class action lawsuit, not individual claims as Ms. Hawkins seeks to do. Perkins v. San Diego Gas & Electric Co. (2014) 225 Cal. App. 4th 492 held that the earlier class action case did not give notice to the defendants of the later individual claims sought to be pursued.

An order that tracks the ruling of the Court with the addition of the parties' agreement is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Not Reported. =(302/HK)

Defendant Harcourt Group, Llc And Sojourn Properties, Inc'S Motion For Stay Of Proceedings Pending Appeal
Off Calendar May-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court cofirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants Harcourt Group And Sojourn Properties, Inc.'s Motion For Stay Of Proceedings Pending Appeal of the order denying class certification is granted. Proceeding with plaintiffs' individual claim prior to the conclusion of the appeal likely would affect the order that is on appeal. The order on appeal was based on, among other factors, whether the individual plaintiffs are proper class representatives and this factor would be materially affected if the individual plaintiffs' claims were litigated prior to the litigation of the class claims. Moreover, litigation of the individual plaintiffs' claims prior to determination of the appeal could result in rulings that are subject to res judicata or collateral estoppel against defendants. If the individual plaintiffs' claims were tried on the merits before the class certification issue is resolved, defendants will be exposed to "one way intervention" which is both unfair and contrary to California law as explained in Home Savings cases. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Plaintiffs Eric Boice, Vinetta Boice And Yowie Stromberg'S Motion For Class Certification
Off Calendar Sep-30-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TYSON REDENBARGER OF THE HOOSHMAND LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-415-318-5709 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. ANDREW N. CONTOPOULOS OF THE BRILLIANT LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-925-274-1400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Eric Boice, Vinetta Boice And Yowie Stromberg'S Motion For Class Certification - The motion for class certification is denied. Individual issues predominate as to the actual damages of each class member and the reasons why each member vacated either one day after moving in or twenty eight days later. An individual action would be better than a class action given these proof issues. An individual has an incentive to bring his/her own claim given the ability to recover statutory and actual damages. Eric Boice and Yowie Stromberg are not proper class representatives because they do not appear to have any relationship with the landlord and were merely living at the premises. The proposed class is overbroad as it dates back to 2008. The statute of limitations is three years pursuant to CCP 338 and the continuing violation doctrine does not apply. That is a doctrine in employment law and cannot be used to revive stale claims in this case. Order signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ajr
